subject: Public Health
title: Life Expectancy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Life Expectancy.

hook.text: In 1900 the average global life expectancy was 31 years. Today it's over 73.

buildup.text: Life expectancy at birth estimates how long a newborn will live based on current death rates.

discovery.text: Most of the gain came from reducing infant and child mortality, not extending old age.

twist.text: Japan leads at 84 years. Some Sub-Saharan nations are still below 55.

climax.text: The gap between richest and poorest countries is over 30 years—a measure of global inequality.

punchline.text: How long you live depends heavily on where you're born.

quiz.question: What mainly drove the dramatic rise in global life expectancy?
